title: Judge Calibration Tracking - Implementation Review

findings.finding: All tests pass 100% across 5 runs (no flakiness detected)
findings.detail: Executed tests 5 times sequentially. All 36 tests passed consistently. No race conditions, timeout issues, or random failures observed.
Schema conforms to JSON Schema draft-2020-12. test_calibration_record_schema_valid passes. All required fields present. Immutability flag enforced on storage.
Schema conforms to JSON Schema. test_disagreement_report_detects_trend validates trend detection (rising/stable/falling). Severity breakdown by confidence bucket implemented.

findings.finding: All error paths emit error_artifact immutably
findings.detail: Checked: measure_calibration exception path, get_judge_disagreement_report exception path, compute_trust_score exception fallback. All call _emit_error_artifact() and store in governance/errors namespace with immutable flag.
Grep scan: enforcement=0, override=0, orchestration=0, closure=0, escalation=0. Code approved.

findings.finding: CalibrationRecord dataclass used correctly with immutable=True
findings.detail: artifact_store.put() called with immutable=True for all calibration records. Records use uuid.uuid4() for calibration_id (unique, immutable). Cannot be overwritten.
measure_calibration() and _get_code_version() correctly capture 'git rev-parse HEAD'. Allows traceability to exact code that produced calibration record.
Confidence bucket boundaries: 0.55->0.5-0.6, 0.60->0.6-0.7, 0.99->0.95-1.0. Division by zero avoided (len(bucket_decisions) always > 0). Trust score clamped to [0,1].

approval_summary: Phase 27 implementation APPROVED. All 36 tests pass 5x, no flakiness. Schemas valid and tested. Error artifacts emitted on all failure paths. Vocabulary safe. Immutability enforced. Source code version tracked. Edge cases handled.
